req.title: Manager creates timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a timesheet through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers UI-driven timesheet creation for managers. I designed tests using EP (valid manager creates timesheet), NEG (missing mandatory fields, unauthenticated, forbidden role), BVA (description at max allowed length), STC (state transition from draft to submitted), and DT (decision table over role x auth combinations). Each test targets /timesheets via Playwright for UI interaction and a lightweight API check for audit record verification. Fixtures and helpers are kept as placeholders to stay within line limits.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_timesheet_with_valid_data
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
No duplicate timesheet exists for the target period
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid equivalence-class values
Query the audit log API for that entity ID
expected: HTTP response status is 200 or 201
Response body contains a non-empty entity ID
Timesheet row appears in the /timesheets list
Audit record exists for the created timesheet
trace: R-WEB-017: audit record written on success
R-WEB-017: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_creates_timesheet_with_valid_data(
    manager_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page as manager
    audit_api_client,    # <fixture>: requests.Session with manager credentials
    valid_timesheet_payload,  # <fixture>: dict of valid mandatory field values
    cleanup_timesheet,   # <fixture>: deletes created timesheet after test
):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/timesheets")
    page.click("[data-testid='btn-create-timesheet']")

    fill_timesheet_form(page, valid_timesheet_payload)  # <helper_fn>
    page.click("[data-testid='btn-submit']")

    success_banner = page.locator("[data-testid='alert-success']")
    expect(success_banner).to_be_visible(timeout=5000)

    entity_id = page.locator("[data-testid='timesheet-id']").inner_text()
    assert entity_id, "Entity ID must be non-empty"
    cleanup_timesheet(entity_id)

    resp = audit_api_client.get(f"/audit?entity_id={entity_id}&action=CREATE")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    records = resp.json().get("records", [])
    assert any(r["entity_id"] == entity_id for r in records), "Audit record missing"

tests.name: test_create_timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
expected: Form submission is blocked or server returns 400
Validation error messages are displayed for each mandatory field
No timesheet entity is created
No audit record is written
trace: R-WEB-017: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_create_timesheet_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400(
    manager_page,       # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page as manager
    api_client_manager, # <fixture>: requests.Session with manager credentials
):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/timesheets")
    page.click("[data-testid='btn-create-timesheet']")

    # Deliberately skip filling any fields
    page.click("[data-testid='btn-submit']")

    error_locators = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error']")
    expect(error_locators.first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
    error_count = error_locators.count()
    assert error_count >= 1, f"Expected validation errors, got {error_count}"

    # Confirm no entity was persisted via API
    resp = api_client_manager.get("/timesheets?sort=created_at:desc&limit=1")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    items = resp.json().get("items", [])
    # Ensure most recent record is not from this test run
    assert_no_incomplete_timesheet_created(items)  # <helper_fn>

tests.name: test_create_timesheet_description_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Max allowed description length is known (e.g. 500 characters)
actions: Fill all mandatory fields; set description to exactly 500 characters
Also submit with 501-character description and verify rejection
expected: 500-character description: form submits, HTTP 200/201, entity created
501-character description: validation error displayed, HTTP 400, no entity created
trace: R-WEB-017: boundary on description field max length
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_LEN = 500

def test_create_timesheet_description_at_max_boundary_length(
    manager_page,
    valid_timesheet_payload,  # <fixture>
    cleanup_timesheet,        # <fixture>
):
    page = manager_page

    # --- At boundary: exactly MAX_LEN chars ---
    page.goto("/timesheets")
    page.click("[data-testid='btn-create-timesheet']")
    payload_max = {**valid_timesheet_payload, "description": "A" * MAX_LEN}
    fill_timesheet_form(page, payload_max)  # <helper_fn>
    page.click("[data-testid='btn-submit']")
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='alert-success']")).to_be_visible(timeout=5000)
    entity_id = page.locator("[data-testid='timesheet-id']").inner_text()
    assert entity_id
    cleanup_timesheet(entity_id)

    # --- Beyond boundary: MAX_LEN + 1 chars ---
    page.goto("/timesheets")
    page.click("[data-testid='btn-create-timesheet']")
    payload_over = {**valid_timesheet_payload, "description": "A" * (MAX_LEN + 1)}
    fill_timesheet_form(page, payload_over)  # <helper_fn>
    page.click("[data-testid='btn-submit']")
    error_loc = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-description']")
    expect(error_loc).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_timesheet
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
actions: Navigate directly to /timesheets without logging in
Attempt to POST to /timesheets API endpoint without auth token
expected: UI redirects to login page or shows 401 error
API returns HTTP 401
No timesheet entity is created
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_timesheet(
    browser_context_no_auth,  # <fixture>: Playwright BrowserContext with no session
    base_url,                 # <fixture>: str, e.g. "https://hr-app.example.com"
    valid_timesheet_payload,  # <fixture>
):
    page = browser_context_no_auth.new_page()
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/timesheets")

    # UI should redirect to login
    expect(page).to_have_url(url_pattern_login(base_url), timeout=5000)  # <helper_fn>

    # Direct API call without token should return 401
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/timesheets",
        json=valid_timesheet_payload,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    page.close()

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_create_timesheet
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not manager)
actions: Authenticate as a user with role 'employee'
Attempt POST to /timesheets API with employee credentials
Navigate to /timesheets in UI and check for create button visibility
expected: API returns HTTP 403
UI does not expose the 'Create Timesheet' button or shows access-denied message
No timesheet entity is created
No audit record is written
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_create_timesheet(
    employee_page,           # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page as employee
    api_client_employee,     # <fixture>: requests.Session with employee credentials
    valid_timesheet_payload, # <fixture>
):
    # UI check: create button must not be present
    page = employee_page
    page.goto("/timesheets")
    create_btn = page.locator("[data-testid='btn-create-timesheet']")
    expect(create_btn).not_to_be_visible(timeout=3000)

    # API check: direct POST must return 403
    resp = api_client_employee.post("/timesheets", json=valid_timesheet_payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Response must contain error detail"

tests.name: test_timesheet_state_transition_draft_to_submitted
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A timesheet in DRAFT state exists for the manager
actions: Click 'Submit' to transition state from DRAFT to SUBMITTED
Verify state change in UI and via API
Verify audit record records the state transition
expected: Timesheet status changes from DRAFT to SUBMITTED
API GET /timesheets/{id} returns status=SUBMITTED
Audit record contains action=STATE_CHANGE with from=DRAFT and to=SUBMITTED
HTTP 200 returned on transition
trace: R-WEB-017: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_timesheet_state_transition_draft_to_submitted(
    manager_page,
    api_client_manager,    # <fixture>
    draft_timesheet_id,    # <fixture>: creates a DRAFT timesheet, returns its ID
    audit_api_client,      # <fixture>
    cleanup_timesheet,     # <fixture>
):
    page = manager_page
    ts_id = draft_timesheet_id
    page.goto(f"/timesheets/{ts_id}")

    status_badge = page.locator("[data-testid='status-badge']")
    expect(status_badge).to_have_text("DRAFT", timeout=3000)

    page.click("[data-testid='btn-submit-timesheet']")
    expect(status_badge).to_have_text("SUBMITTED", timeout=5000)

    resp = api_client_manager.get(f"/timesheets/{ts_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["status"] == "SUBMITTED"

    audit_resp = audit_api_client.get(
        f"/audit?entity_id={ts_id}&action=STATE_CHANGE"
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    records = audit_resp.json().get("records", [])
    transition = next((r for r in records if r.get("to") == "SUBMITTED"), None)
    assert transition is not None, "State-change audit record missing"
    assert transition["from"] == "DRAFT"
    cleanup_timesheet(ts_id)
